Don Crowther
Any time I start a new program
I like to set a goal...
A goal specifically focused on
what I intend to get out of this
particular program
So since this program is focused on
creating a high-converting webinar, to
put dollars in your bank account, I
thought we’d call this process

-- 1 of 7 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2
So, I’m Going To Ask You To Create A
Goal
• Of how much you’re going to make from a webinar
within the next 12 weeks
• And while I’d love it if every one of you was able to make a
million dollars or more in the next 12 weeks from your
webinar, I invite you to create a more realistic goal
Let’s look at a few factors
4
5
6
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 3
How Have You Done In The Past?
• How many webinars have you run in the past?
• How did your best one (that didn’t involve traffic coming
from an outside source) do (in dollars)?
• How many people were you able to get to register for that
webinar?
• How many attended?
What Will You Sell?
• Do you have a product ready to sell via a webinar right
now?
• If you don’t, how soon could you have a product ready to
sell?
• Could you do a webinar for someone else’s product?
• Could you eventually make money by creating and/or
running webinars for other people’s businesses?
How Big Is Your List?
• How many people do you currently have on your list who
would reasonably be able to buy the product you want to
sell on your webinar?
• How many people (considering your current open and
click-through rates) can you reasonably expect to register
for your webinar?
7
8
9
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 7 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 4
What’s Your Budget?
• How much could you spend to advertise your webinar?
• How much time could you devote to creating your
webinar?
I’m sure you can see that there are
lots of things to consider as you set a
goal for your next webinar
Considering all of those factors
How much do you reasonably
think you could make from a
webinar in the next 12 weeks?
10
11
12

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 5
Don’t know? May I make a
suggestion?
$2,000
That’s Just About
• 10 sales of a $197 product
• 5 sales of a $497 product
• 2 sales of a $997 product
• 1 sale of a $1997 product
• (Offering a 3-payment option on any of these will push
your sales to over $2000)
All of those sales numbers look reasonable to me
And I’m sure you recognize the
relationship between what you paid
for this product and that goal...

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 6
So, what’s your number?
Write it down
And while you’re at it
I suggest you also create a
stretch goal
Stretch Goals
• Something still possible, but that stretch you and your
efforts a bit further
• They’re a great way to make sure that you’re always
pushing to go beyond, to expand, to achieve
Key: possible but not easy to achieve
